title: /dev/tty0 is not available in rootful system containers
body:
### Issue Description

I need tty0 in a systemd container to run Xorg.
But tty0 dispears in the container, if the ENTRYPOINT or CMD is /usr/sbin/init.

I also tried docker-ce, and it works as I expected(tty0 is available in docker-ce systemd containers)

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Start a rootful container, with/without ENTRYPOINT set to /usr/sbin/init
2. In the container, run "ls /dev“ and see if tty0 is available


### Describe the results you received

Please see below for the with/without comparison.
You can simply copy the bold commandline to reproduce.

[root@fugui ~]# **docker kill local**
local
[root@fugui ~]# **podman run --name=local -tid --rm --privileged --entrypoint=/usr/sbin/init centos:7.9.2009** 
07f045af2562c0085760b749d75dff8e88cf7479a3d5bd0d9a7ce1081080919b
[root@fugui ~]# **podman exec local ls /dev | grep tty0 -i** 

[root@fugui ~]# **podman kill local**
local
[root@fugui ~]# **podman run --name=local -tid --rm --privileged centos:7.9.2009** 
5c3dffe4288031b75be27d62ec4babb014411fe36f614145b3ab5ddb2c580f49
[root@fugui ~]# **podman exec local ls /dev | grep tty0 -i** 
tty0


### Describe the results you expected

**podman exec local ls /dev | grep tty0 -i**  should always return **tty0**, no matter if systemd is enabled.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Baremetal

### Additional information

[root@fugui ~]# cat /etc/containers/containers.conf

comments.body: This is intentional; see #15895. It might be possible to work around this with a `--device` command-line option, can you see if that works for you?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
No response, we can assume this has been resolved.
